programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Retirees and childhood sweethearts Liz and Warren have been together for over half a century, but a relaxed lifestyle is the last thing on their minds. They've taken on a hundred-acre Macadamia farm in the Byron Bay hinterland, with grand plans to build an epic five-bedroom Mediterranean style villa-cum-castle. Inspired by their adventures abroad their design is whimsical, unconventional, and extravagant. With no architect their plans embrace spontaneity and change on a whim, add in project managing it themselves and things soon freewheel. Question is, will this fairy tale dream have a happy ending?

programme.title: Grand Designs UK
programme.desc: Duncan and Liz build a revolutionary eco home in the picturesque Cotswolds. It will not only use very little energy, it'll also produce four times the energy it needs - effectively a mini-power station.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Australian Idol voice-coach Erana Clarke and her two kids love their home on Sydney's northern beaches. None of them wants to leave however a divorce has left Erana with little choice. The home presents well at the front, but the back is a nightmare that sends buyers running for the door. The garden is a sloping jungle of weeds, the bedrooms are dark and dingy and the laundry is a war zone. The house was originally listed at offers over $900,000, and when the Selling Houses Australia renovations are complete Erana wants even more! Will buyers fork out $925,000 plus, or has Erana priced herself out of the market?

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: This is a luxury house on a new estate, but nobody is buying. If Lisa and her husband Jamie don't sell the house, they will be bankrupt. The Evans family have had more than their fair share of bad luck. Lisa has beaten two bouts of breast cancer and a divorce has left her with a mountain of legal bills. The house may be luxurious, however, it lacks soul. The stark white interior has precious little furniture and the backyard is a desolate wasteland. After two years on the market, the family is desperate. Can we turn this house into a home and will the Evans family get their asking price come auction day?

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team show you how to create the definitive secret garden, full of bright and colourful plants, hidden corners and magical touches for a little girl who uses a wheelchair and her family.

programme.title: Christmas At Stately Home
programme.desc: Almost half of Blenheim's preservation and restoration bill is covered by Christmas takings. So for the palace's army of staff and specialist teams, the pressure is on to make the event triumph.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Biscuit Week and the bakers make Viennese sandwiches, a retro minty favourite and dramatic puppet theatres. Who'll take centre stage, and who's delivered their final act?

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Kiell Smith-Bynoe returns for Reduce Reuse Recycle week. The sewers make quilted bags from scraps, create new items from textile waste, and repurpose wedding dresses into party outfits.

programme.title: Celebrity Sewing Bee Festive Specials
programme.desc: The four celebrities taking part in 2024's Christmas special are: Toyah Willcox, Kerry Godliman, Jessica Knappett and Hammed Animashaun.

programme.title: Inside The Ambulance
programme.desc: The blue lights are flashing as crews attend a cycling accident and a man having a suspected stroke. The paramedics assist a woman who's fallen and a baby who has had multiple seizures.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Emergency
programme.desc: Twenty-four-year-old Stanlie is airlifted after he is impaled on a metal pole on a construction site. Max and his two-year-old daughter Darcy are rushed to A&E after their van collides with a bus.

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: The paramedics treat a man who's overdosed on drugs, a woman who's losing blood fast and an elderly man who is confused and may have had a mini-stroke.

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Nearing the end of her training Alex is allowed to assist in complex cases. Here she meets Holly with a rare split womb who's attempting her sixth round of IVF with her husband Mark.

programme.title: Home Greek Home
programme.desc: The build is under pressure, with only five weeks to go until tourist season begins. With builders doubling up as speedboat drivers and noisy building work banned in the summer months, Sarah is up against the clock.

programme.desc: After renting the local schoolhouse for 20 years, Chatham Islanders John and Bridget Preece are finally embarking on their own dream build.

programme.desc: Coming to stay in the Chiltern Hills Countryside are Ursula and her design term from Berlin, Germany. With their many questions it seems unlikely host Penny will be able to make a swift exit.

programme.desc: The potters are back at the wheel, tasked to make their own range of cookware as the battle of clay continues. Judge Rich Miller sets a second terracotta challenge to engrave tiles.

programme.title: Location Location Location
programme.desc: This time, we're catching up with two sets of house hunters whose searches were all about family. In 2018, retired couple Sue and Tom made the difficult decision to sell their beloved country cottage in Tom's native Yorkshire and move south to be closer to Sue's grandson Freddie. After their house sold in just six days, they found themselves living in their motorhome- and winter was coming, Phil took them on a voyage of discovery through the east of England, where it was as much about finding the perfect garden as the perfect house. Two years later, Phil has returned to see whether their relocation reaped rewards. In 2019, recently divorced dad Charlie was back living with his parents whilst he searched for a new home near Maidstone. Keen to provide space and stability for his children Rosie and Hugo, green fingered Charlie was looking for a house with a large garden - but a tight budget and stock-poor search area was making his search nigh on impossible. Having lost out on several properties, Charlie was all at sea, and needed Kirstie's help to find safe harbour. A year on, we're back to see whether his family found dry land.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts another fun packed episode of the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie along with hilarious guest performer Keegan Michael Key. programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts another hilarious episode of the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie along with guest performer Keegan Michael Key and star guest Candice Accola from the CW's Vampire Diaries. Highlights include a succession of bizarre suggestions for what mysterious props might be, Hollywood director Colin steering the others through a very weird alien movie, Keegan, Ryan, Colin and Wayne in the world's worst Dating Profiles and Candice joining in the fun by acting as a succession of props in a Mount Everest climbing scene before being serenaded by Latin love god Wayne.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers. It is vast, untamed, and home to some of the toughest to reach places on Earth. For the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, or braving a continent's worth of brutal bitumen and desolate dirt, this is a land that can destroy trucks and truckers in a heartbeat. But for the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again, for a season of dirt, dramas and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers. It is vast, untamed, and home to some of the toughest to reach places on Earth. For the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, or braving a continent's worth of brutal bitumen and desolate dirt, this is a land that can destroy trucks and truckers in a heartbeat. But for the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again, for a season of dirt, dramas and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out.

programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts another fun packed episode of the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie along with hilarious guest performer Keegan Michael Key. programme.desc: Back in the city of Orange, the Vegas Ladies arrive in vintage style, though not everyone agrees; Casey finds the chutzpah to pick up a good unit; Darrell tries to peddle his wares to Emily.

programme.title: Junior Taskmaster
programme.desc: Strap yourselves in because it's the first semi final. Our contestants meet Mark the Milkman for the first time, and perform poetry, while displaying varying levels of drama.

programme.title: Bookie
programme.desc: While Lorraine takes Walt on a spiritual business trip, Hector recruits Danny and Ray to help collect on a soccer bet from a notorious drug lord.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan and Charlie both wake up next to a weird woman, leading them to try to figure out what happened the night before.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Chelsea invites her college friend to stay at the house after she has a bad break-up.

programme.title: Truck Night In America
programme.desc: This program makes history with the toughest truck obstacle course ever built. America and trucks go together and, since 1925 when the first American factory-produced truck rolled off the line, American owners have been devising ingenious ways to make them faster, stronger, and better. Five truck owners will compete in a series of gruelling challenges that test their craftsmanship and driving ability to the max. Each episode features unique challenges that test trucks in a way that's never been seen before. The challenges culminate in The Green Hell, a three-mile obstacle course which includes challenges that send trucks flying off jumps, over a mountain of crushed cars and even through a snake-infested swamp. Helping them along the way are four experts in the off-road world - they coach, they build, they fix, but the one thing they can't do is drive. In the end, the truck who finishes is crowned the champion - if they can make it that far. In this episode, an epic line-up features a 2000 horsepower Dodge pick-up competing against a 1971 Land Rover. In a never-before-seen twist on the tug-o-war, the Silo Pulldown dumps a big surprise on the losers. And one challenge hits hard, forcing the medics to rush in.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: There's trouble in the wind for Kurt Shepherd who is struggling with a load causing oversized problems. For Johnny Dowell on the Tanami Track it's too hard one minute and too soft the next.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: In Michigan, a late collector's home features a stockpile of awesome wheels. And a wide-eyed Mike comes up with a staggering six-figure offer - the largest in American Pickers history - to take home five ultra-rare motorbikes. Meanwhile, Danielle nails a shot-in-the-dark mission for rock'n'roll legend Jack White and then it's up to Robbie and Dave to transport a legendary recording trailer to its new home in Detroit.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Winds in excess of 48km/h batter Desert Hot Springs, and the buyers do their best to keep standing; Rene and Gunther dust off an old locker; Kenny steps up his game.

Along the way we meet some of Australia's most authentic and colourful characters, whose stories reveal a rich Australian history, and what the pickers find will surprise and astound you.

programme.title: Spectacular Spider-Man

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: Spawn's nightmares reveal that Al Simmons' killer was his mercenary ex-partner, Chapel. CIA chief Jason Wynn tells Chapel to find a cache of stolen guns before they are uncovered and traced back to him.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Physically and mentally debilitated after the four-month ordeal on Guadalcanal, Leckie, Basilone and thousands of their comrades land in Melbourne, where they are greeted by adoring crowds and viewed as the saviours of Australia. While his buddies carouse, Leckie becomes deeply attached to an Australian woman and her first-generation Greek family. Meanwhile, Basilone is awarded the Medal of Honour and is asked to return home to help sell US war bonds.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Finally enlisted as a marine, Sledge trains for combat at Camp Elliott. The 1st Marine Division lands at Cape Gloucester on the Japanese-held island of New Britain. As Leckie and the other marines battle the Japanese, they quickly realise that the more ominous enemy is the smothering jungle itself. Having survived Gloucester and being stationed on the godforsaken island of Pavuvu, Leckie begins displaying the physical and mental effects of combat and is sent to a naval hospital on nearby Banika for psychiatric observation.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Basilone's celebrity grows as he travels across the country on the war bonds tour. On Pavuvu, Sledge, assigned to the 5th Marines, 1st Marine Division, is briefly reunited with Phillips and Leckie rejoins his company. Sledge then gets his first taste of combat as he, Leckie, and the rest of the 1st Marine Division meet fierce Japanese resistance while landing on the intricately and heavily defended coral island of Peleliu.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Despite the suffocating 115-degree heat and a lack of clean drinking water, Sledge, Leckie and the other Marines confront the highly-fortified enemy as they attempt to capture the Peleliu airfield. After seeing his comrades badly injured, Leckie himself is wounded and evacuated from the island. Sledge witnesses the shocking truth about what is sometimes required to survive and fight another day.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: The Marines, including the newly christened 'Sledgehammer', continue the battle of Peleliu against an enemy determined to fight to the last man. Devastated by the loss of a revered leader, and witnessing unimaginable barbarity on both sides, Sledge veers to the very edge of moral collapse. Their objective finally secured, the Marines return to Pavuvu fundamentally changed by their experience on Peleliu.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Increasingly frustrated by his role campaigning for war bonds, Basilone convinces the marines to allow him to train troops headed for combat. Transferred to Camp Pendleton, he enjoys a whirlwind romance with an initially reluctant female marine, Lena Riggi. But the couple know they are living on borrowed time, as Basilone is soon to take part in the marine-landing on Iwo Jima.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: After battling across the island of Okinawa for over a month, Sledge and the rest of the 1st Marine Division are ordered to relieve an Army division that has been in combat against the most strongly defended Japanese position on the island. The primordial conditions and the moral dilemma posed by the presence of civilians puts tremendous strain on the physical and psychological endurance of Sledge and the other Marines.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: After the Japanese surrender, Leckie, healed from his wounds, leaves the hospital and returns home, while Sledge heads back to Alabama to be reunited with his family and Sid Phillips. Lena visits Basilone's home and has an emotional meeting with his family. Leckie adjusts to post-war life by resuming his old job and starting a new relationship, but for Sledge, unsure why he survived the war seemingly unscathed, adjustment will require more time.

programme.title: John From Cincinnati
programme.desc: Set in Imperial Beach, California, the last great surf-break before Tijuana, this is a story about the Yosts: a family of surfers whose awesome athletic talents have seemed for generations to come with a curse attached. The Yosts' reign and reputation has been eroded by years of bad luck, addiction and hubris and, just as things are looking like they can't get worse, a stranger named John arrives, and the Yosts' banal existence is lifted into something profound, miraculous and, possibly, universal.

programme.desc: As German officers hand over their weapons, soldiers raid well-stocked wine cellars, snag souvenirs from residences, and endure one final tragedy when a replacement soldier cracks. But as the men compare "points" to see who has earned enough to go home, word arrives that the Japanese have surrendered, ending the war and dissolving Easy Company. A memorable closing vignette tells what happened to Winters and some of his men after they returned to the States.

programme.desc: When the corpse of a man is found inside a tomb dating back from the Civil War, the team discovers that the victim was a sergeant who was buried alive. Meanwhile, Ziva becomes a full-time NCIS agent.

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Nell joins the team in the field, after they discover that the ransom demand for a former Navy officer's girlfriend, who has been kidnapped, is intelligence information, while Deeks remains unaware that he has broken one of Hetty's rules.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: When a public defender is murdered, Cosgrove and Shaw are surprised to find Price at the crime scene. His involvement in the trial compromises the case.

programme.title: The Closer
programme.desc: Brenda's parents, Cliff and Willie Rae, visit Brenda, trying to get her to pin down her wedding date. When a murder investigation interrupts the visit, Brenda can't let go of the case and brings her parents along to investigate. Will Brenda slow down when the health of someone close to home is in jeopardy?

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The CSI team inspects a twisted trail of evidence when a saleswoman washes up on Rockaway Beach along with a dead shark.

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: When a guidance counsellor is murdered at a private school, the mayor orders Mac and his team to drop everything to find out what happened.

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A grizzly bear attack in the everglades puts suspicion on the hunting party, but things get more complicated once their hotel room is found.

programme.title: FBI: Most Wanted

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: When a young woman dies a slow and painful death from an untreated infection, Cosgrove and Shaw suspect she was under the influence of a powerful cult.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a non-commissioned officer is shot dead on the freeway, the NCIS team take on the investigation. When it appears that the victim was leading a double life, it throws the investigation in a new direction.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a Marine's wife is murdered live on the internet, the investigation reveals that two housewives were running a live internet sex site and are now both missing.

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: When a joint FBI/CIA/NCIS raid goes bad, the team suspects a mole. It turns out to be the work of a hacker with evidence leading to a 15-year-old girl. Realising the girl's hacking program has been stolen, Callen goes undercover.

programme.desc: The body of someone extremely overweight turns up in the trunk of a car - the body of a man who stole $3 million in diamonds from his own jewellery store. programme.desc: When a six-year-old boy informs the NCIS team that his father was kidnapped, the team discover that the missing father is actually a Lt Commander working on a secret project named Honor.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ziva and Tony spend time together in a hotel as they go undercover, assuming the identities of hired assassins who are now dead. Gibbs soon discovers that his team is not the only one working the case.

programme.title: Cold Case
programme.desc: After bank robberies by thieves wearing Johnny Cash masks, Rush finds a link that may give new information on an old murder of a bank teller during an almost identical robbery.

programme.title: Cold Case
programme.desc: When a drug case is reopened, Valens reveals he had worked undercover on the high-profile case, and now he must face the demons that have been haunting him for eight years.

programme.title: FBI
programme.desc: After a suburban woman is kidnapped from her home, the team look into trysts on both sides of a seemingly perfect marriage; OA feels pressure from Mona to make changes in his career path.

programme.title: FBI
programme.desc: The team must track down a shooter targeting detectives from the same precinct and unit, while facing mounting tension from the NYPD.

programme.title: NCIS: Hawai'i
programme.desc: Tennant and her NCIS unit must take down a notorious team of international thieves using WWII explosives on armoured trucks before they complete their final big job and disappear.

programme.title: NCIS: Hawai'i
programme.desc: Kai goes undercover with one of Hawaii's oldest surf gangs when a petty officer, who's trying to help wayward kids find a new path in the Marines, is murdered.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Solo Peanut, A Social Butterfly And The Truth
programme.desc: Paige visits Sheldon's university, stunned that Sheldon has friends in Oscar and Darren, when she is lonely at her own school. She later returns and asks to hideout in Sheldon's dorm room, thinking of dropping out. Missy finds out and tells Mary. Paige is gone when they arrive but thanks Sheldon for helping her. She returned to her mother a few days later. Connie goes drinking with Mary, pretending she is fine with Dale dumping her. At the store, Dale annoys George by asking questions about Connie. Meanwhile, Georgie tells Mandy his true age and she throws him out. Mandy confronts Connie who in turn makes Georgie realize what he did was wrong. The older Sheldon narrates he realizes now parenting is hard as he raises his own children. Georgie goes to talk to Mandy again, who reveals she is pregnant.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam jeopardises his relationship with Jackie when he gives Barry and Erica an advance look at the yearbook. Murray and Beverly square off when his brother Marvin needs help.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As their family and friends struggle to accept that Erica and Lainey are graduating from high school, Adam panics when he's caught between his old girlfriend and his new one.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Amy convince the Captain to let them work a case together involving a street drug, but their new relationship gets in the way.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Terry's wife goes into labour sooner than expected. An internet outage forces Charles and Amy to process all paperwork by hand, and Holt has an encounter with an ex-boyfriend.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: A mother-to-be meets Alan and Walden when she's looking for suitable parents to adopt her baby.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden becomes a foster parent to a six-year-old boy, but worries about himself when they don't hit it off like he hoped.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan breaks Walden's rules when he realises that Louis thinks he's cool, and he wants it stay that way.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Elaine has a blind date with the guy from her wake-up service who likes dogs. A bit of grapefruit pulp from Jerry's healthy breakfast gets into George's eye and causes problems for him when his winks keep getting misinterpreted.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George picks up a bad habit from some visiting baseball officials. Elaine has an out of country runner as her house guest during the New York City marathon. The runner overslept and missed the big race at the last Olympics and Jerry obsesses over ensuring that it doesn't happen again. Kramer gets a hot tub from his friend Lomez and Elaine has writer's block.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie gets upset because one of her fellow secretaries "steals her moxie" and gets a promotion, and everything at IPS falls apart after Doug temporarily takes over the office.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Deacon and Kelly don't want Doug around Major any more after he inadvertently scares him, and Arthur begins a new business selling senior citizen services.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Suitcase Full Of Cash And A Yellow Clown Car
programme.desc: Connie has a lot of money from her gaming room. Unable to put it in the bank, she gives a few hundred dollars to Sheldon and Missy. Sheldon buys three shares in RadioShack, though his ideas for the business don't get very far. He later admits his idea was similar to the attempted Incredible Universe. Missy buys a cotton candy machine and Georgie tells her how to make a profit by selling the candy, though ants later ruin the machine. Dale suggests they take the rest of the money to retire and travel together, but Connie buys a yellow Mazda Miata sports car. Meanwhile, it appears George will be replaced as football coach. Sheldon's college no longer has a football team and no other coaching options are close to where the family lives. Dale offers to let George work in his store on a trial run, and may let George takeover the store eventually. With Connie unwilling to slow down in life, Dale breaks up with her.

programme.desc: Durst's interview with Jarecki begins with his childhood, when he witnessed his mother's suicide. His father, Seymour, pressures young Robert to join the family business and Durst describes his disinterest in following in his father's footsteps. He recalls meeting Kathie McCormack, a beautiful girl from a modest background. The couple fall in love and move to Vermont, where they lead a simple life and run a health-food store called All Good Things. But not long after, Seymour prevails and Robert returns to New York to take his place at the powerful Durst Organisation.

programme.title: Secrets of Penthouse
programme.desc: As Penthouse enters the 1990s, the huge success of the magazine has led Bob Guccione to embark on a series of costly investments, including cryogenics and a casino in Atlantic City which is eventually sold to Donald Trump. Bob's youngest son Nick persuades his dad to let him branch out into video production. But the venture doesn't last and Bob pushes Nick out of the company which takes an emotional toll on Nick. Meanwhile Bob's relationship with his Penthouse 'Pets' takes a dark turn when Anneka DiLorenzo, a former 'Pet', takes Bob to court, accusing him of exploiting her as a sex slave.

programme.title: Secrets of Penthouse
programme.desc: As Penthouse enters the 2000s, the magazine is in trouble. With sales falling, Bob Guccione pushes for the magazine to go harder against the advice of those around him, and Penthouse loses advertisers. Bob has pushed his three sons out of the business and taps his daughter Nina to help run the business as the age of the internet dawns. Adult superstars and Penthouse 'Pets' Jenna Jameson and Sunny Leone are forging their own paths in the adult entertainment business, becoming huge stars online. After the death of his long-time partner Kathy Keaton, Bob's debts continue to mount, and he is forced to sell his New York mansion and private art collection. In 2010, Bob passes away following an illness. His youngest son Nick visits Bob's grave for the first time.

programme.title: Europe's Waterways
programme.sub-title: France - From The Camargue To The Ardeche
programme.desc: Venturing upstream on Europe's waterways, the series discovers five regions of the continent from an unknown perspective and visits people who live from, on and with water. Rivers, canals, lake regions: they have shaped Europe's cultures for centuries - they are a place to live, transport arteries and sources of energy. Five journeys on watercourses broad and narrow lead to Italy, Germany, France, Poland, Holland and Belgium. Along the way: new projects for the future of water in Europe.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star

programme.title: The Canary Islands
programme.sub-title: La Palma - The Galapagos Of Europe
programme.desc: Off the African coast in the middle of the Atlantic, underwater volcanoes raged for millions of years and created a volcanic archipelago: the Canary Islands. Each of them has its own face. This series tells of natural spectacles, unique natural areas and the formation of the volcanic archipelago.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick Stein approaches the end of his meander through France with a final glance at the culinary delights of the Languedoc before crossing over to Provence, and the Mediterranean coastline that continues to reveal new insights into southern French cuisine. In the border town of Uzes, Rick rediscovers his love for salt cod and marvels at the colourful array of French patisserie as he enjoys a seasonal fig tart with his morning coffee. He then heads towards the harbour town of Cassis, not for seafood, but for North African lamb chorba cooked by a local fisherman.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts

programme.title: Jamie's Quick And Easy Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie applies his quick and easy principles to the festive season, focusing on genius combinations of just 5 ingredients to create most fabulous Christmas feast. Jamie's Quick & Easy Christmas is brilliantly simple and delivers utterly delicious results with all of the flavour but none of the fuss. It's the easy way to an impressive Christmas. This year, Jamie is cooking up his Quick & Easy Christmas at home, creating 10 brand new festive recipes for his family and close friends, and joined in the kitchen from time to time by his very little helpers: Petal, Buddy and River,

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: Chef Michael van de Elzen and restaurateur Ganesh Raj have a battle on their hands to persuade this family addicted to takeaway food and energy drinks to eat well for less.

programme.title: James Martin's Christmas Comforts
programme.desc: James shares a stress-free plan for the big day, pulling together the perfect Christmas meal for a crowd of his friends. Special guest is Paul Ainsworth.

programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: These soothing and much loved home comfort delights range from the very simple to the extremely decadent. Mary indulges her love of comfort food with homely recipes to warm the heart, including eggs Benedict Florentine (why make one when you can cook both), fall-off-the-bone slow-cooked lamb shanks and deliciously rich chocolate truffle pots. She invites Swedish fire-chef Niklas Esktedt to share the joys of cave-man cooking with her, and finds herself outside by an open fire enjoying his inventive venison meatballs with blackened celeriac and mouth-watering Swedish doughnuts.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon shows us how to make his favourite street food dishes at home. Recipes include delicious prawn tostada, irresistible caramel salted popcorn and his son Jack helps make buttermilk fried chicken, a Ramsay family favourite.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie is sharing four more mouthwatering recipes. His succulent lamb stew is so quick to prep, but with seriously deep flavours; and a fast and furious tasty warm lentil salad.

programme.title: Jamie At Home Christmas
programme.desc: In this special, Jamie takes the strain out of Christmas dinner and allows you to reclaim your Christmas day. By following Jamie's advice you can be calm, organised and prepared and your Christmas dinner will be a sure-fire winner. Good ingredients are paramount and Jamie has a visit from his geese and turkey suppliers who advise us what to look for in a good bird. Jamie and Brian, his gardener, look at the greens on offer in the garden and Gennaro, Jamie's Italian mentor, drops in to roast some chestnuts. Jamie walks us through the elements of the perfect roast dinner and tips on how to prepare and cook your birds, how to roast the perfect potatoes, dress the perfect greens and make sensational mince pies. By following Jamie's advice and tips your Christmas culinary preparations should be stress-free forever more.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Christmas Dinner
programme.desc: It's Tom's favourite meal of the year and with clever planning and a passion for making everything taste as good as it can be, the result is a wonderful festive table laden with amazing food to delight family and friends.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: To keep their feet nice and warm, the British spend almost 723 million pounds a year on socks! Gregg Wallace visits a sock factory in Leicester that produces one and a half million socks annually. This atmospheric factory is a fascinating mix of Wallace and Gromit-style and high-tech machinery, with a close-knit team of highly skilled operators. Elsewhere in the episode, Cherry Healey finds out what causes smelly feet and pounds the pavements to test out which socks best tackle this stinky situation. She visits a cotton spinning factory in Manchester, whose high-tech machinery produces 4,200 miles of yarn every hour, and meets a revolutionary eco cotton supplier. Historian Ruth Goodman steps back into the 1980s, when socks were at the height of fashion. And she ventures further back to the Great War, when the Kitchener stitch helped to save the feet of British soldiers in the trenches.

programme.desc: This signature challenge is to bake one family-sized savoury quiche with a filling of their choice. The technical bake is a pistachio frangipane tart with roasted rhubarb and raspberries.

After Gino led them to the deserts of Morocco for last year's mis-adventure, Gordon is determined that they celebrate the season properly this time, so has arranged a boys trip away before the madness of a family xmas kicks in, to the ultimate winter wonderland and the official home of Santa. programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: This is classic Mary at her best as she returns to school to celebrate her light, easy and delicious cooking. From a simple version of the classic kedgeree and some delightful breakfast drop scones, to a moreish chicken, avocado and bacon salad and deliciously easy beef burgers. Mary takes her inspiration from a visit to a groundbreaking primary school in South London who has built a secret garden where they teach their students how to grow vegetables and look after a brood of chickens. It doesn't stop there, as Mary joins two enthusiastic and skilful year five pupils in the teaching kitchen to make something deliciously fresh for their school lunch.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon shows us his favourite recipes for an Italian breakfast, lunch, and dinner. His son, Jack, gets down to work, helping to cook crostini, beef pappardelle, and sensational panna cotta with hazelnut brittle.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie's cooking chilli con carne meatballs, plus sticky kicking chicken, watermelon radish salad and crunchy noodles.

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat Christmas

programme.title: Gino's Italian Christmas Feast
programme.desc: Spend Christmas with Gino and the D'Acampo family and friends. This one-off special is a celebration of Italian food and traditions, culminating with a feast of all of Gino's favourites.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Cake artist Michelle, must bring a legend to life for her next commission when she's tasked with creating a 2 feet tall fearsome dragon cake, complete with striking rice paper wings, as a surprise 50th birthday. Eloise gets fruity with her latest order, as she attempts to make a zebra striped cake, surrounded by ultra-realistic fruit shaped and flavoured mini-cakes for a music festival. Karisha tackles a very challenging wedding cake for her best friend, which sees her hand placing over 10,000 sugar gems onto a towering 4-foot tall cake, so each tier looks like it's bursting open like a 'rock geode', shimmering with white crystals.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits the Denby factory in Derbyshire, which has been making pottery since 1809. We Britsdrink a staggering 195 million mugs of tea and coffee every day, so Gregg is following production of one of the factory's best sellers, the Halo Heritage mug.

programme.desc: The Great Festive Bake Off sees Noel, Sandi, Prue and Paul recovered from Christmas and reunited in the tent. Together they'll see in the New Year with Britain's biggest boyband, The London Gay Men's Chorus.

programme.desc: Gordon turns his focus to cooking amazing food for parties at home. His eldest daughter Megan helps cook a shoulder of pulled pork with chipotle mayonnaise, perfect for making any party go with a swing.

programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: Mary embraces the British countryside with fresh and honest bucolic cooking inspired by what we grow on our farms and gardens - from a joyous pasta inspired by a classic French herb sauce, to a perfect way to partner asparagus and how to serve up the most scrumptious roasted vegetables. Mary makes her way through our vegetable rows and orchards, ending with her own inspired version of the delicious tarte tatin. To further explore the green fields, she takes a step back in time to journey on one of the Watercress Line's 19th-century steam trains, where she leaves the stove to help fire up the 300-ton engine.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas With Bells On
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver provides an inspiring collection of traditional Christmas classics, alongside new ideas and fantastic party food.

programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: Mary has always adored entertaining and pulls out some of her finest classics that will leave any guest begging for the next invitation - be it a romantic meal for two or hosting a big meal for friends and family. From her divine Dover sole cooked to perfection, a genius way to make beetroot gravadlax and a sticky chicken recipe that will have them knocking down the doors, to an ambitious crispy roast duck and a simple but exquisite raspberry mousse. For a look at the extreme ways one can decorate to entertain, Mary travels to Leeds Castle with acclaimed florist Simon Lycett as he prepares for the Festival of Flowers. Bringing Henry VIII's banquet hall to life, he shows Mary that too much is in fact never enough.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon shows us some of the American-inspired dishes he loves cooking for his family at home. Recipes include eggs, bacon, and hash browns, superb caesar salad, and irresistible peanut butter and jam cookies.

programme.desc: Jamie cooks crispy camembert parcels, autumn salad and cranberry dip, plus golden chicken, braised greens and potato gratin.

programme.desc: Nigella returns for a very special Christmas treat when she travels to Amsterdam to enjoy the festive season. Nigella takes viewers on visits to her favourite places in the Dutch capital city.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits the Ercol factory in Buckinghamshire, an area associated with furniture making since the 19th century. Brits spend a staggering 300 million pounds each week on furniture, and Gregg is following the production of one of this factory's best sellers, the Windsor chair. Starting life as oak and walnut trees from European woodlands, they're cut, drilled, steamed, curved and sanded until they're ready for delivery to shops and homes. Meanwhile, Cherry Healey investigates how sitting too much could be very bad for our health, and she helps to manage a sustainable woodland at the Rushmore Estate in Wiltshire. And historian Ruth Goodman discovers how utility furniture made during the Blitz is still influencing the designs we buy today, as well as learning how a humble British carpenter went on to make the most expensive piece of furniture ever sold.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt and Maggie are taking the bakers back in time for retro week. The bakers must bake a Swiss roll within two hours in the signature challenge, and a Neenish tart for the technical bake.

programme.desc: Jamie prepares some exciting alternatives to traditional Christmas desserts with a little help from his mum, Sally, including a killer chocolate mousse and a festive strudel.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Veggie Christmas
programme.desc: Tom takes us step-by-step through his meat-free menu, including his stunning 'Cheesemas' parcel, crispy potato cakes and white chocolate trifle.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off: Festive Specials
programme.desc: Noel, Sandi, Prue and Paul are back in the tent for their traditional Christmas get-together. They'll round off Christmas with legendary British rock band Status Quo.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon shows us some of the American-inspired dishes he loves cooking for his family at home. Recipes include eggs, bacon, and hash browns, superb caesar salad, and irresistible peanut butter and jam cookies.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie cooks crispy camembert parcels, autumn salad and cranberry dip, plus golden chicken, braised greens and potato gratin.

programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: Mary has always adored entertaining and pulls out some of her finest classics that will leave any guest begging for the next invitation - be it a romantic meal for two or hosting a big meal for friends and family. From her divine Dover sole cooked to perfection, a genius way to make beetroot gravadlax and a sticky chicken recipe that will have them knocking down the doors, to an ambitious crispy roast duck and a simple but exquisite raspberry mousse. For a look at the extreme ways one can decorate to entertain, Mary travels to Leeds Castle with acclaimed florist Simon Lycett as he prepares for the Festival of Flowers. Bringing Henry VIII's banquet hall to life, he shows Mary that too much is in fact never enough.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Cookbook
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver has been cooking Christmas for his family for 20 years. In that time, he's experimented with loads of different ways of cooking the classics. But this year, he wants to show us his ultimate recipes - the ones he's decided really are the very best for Christmas. These are the dishes he'll be cooking on the big day for his own family this year.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Shortcuts
programme.desc: Jamie's recipes for a spectacular Christmas feast: the ultimate roast turkey, a show-stopping veggie centrepiece, and a stuffing to please both meat-eaters and vegetarians.

programme.title: Mary Makes Christmas
programme.desc: Mary brings festive cheer with delicious holiday recipes, from classic mince pies to show-stopping desserts. Join her for expert tips, festive fun, and mouthwatering seasonal delights.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The bakers are challenged to make savoury buns and bread horns filled with delectable delights. In the Technical challenge, the bakers must follow along as Paul Hollywood demonstrates a recipe.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas With Bells On
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver provides an inspiring collection of traditional Christmas classics, alongside new ideas and fantastic party food.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: The budget is blown, the cupboards are overflowing, and treats are just the norm. Will Michael and Ganesh's intervention cut back some seriously extravagant shopping as they try to get them to eat well for less.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Shortcuts
programme.desc: Jamie's recipes for a spectacular Christmas feast: the ultimate roast turkey, a show-stopping veggie centrepiece, and a stuffing to please both meat-eaters and vegetarians.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Phil and Christine take on a very unusual commission as they make a cake to celebrate a friend's recovery from a broken ankle, and devise a leg shaped cake, complete with a removable, modelling chocolate plaster cast. Michelle makes an edible work of art for her village's annual art's festival in the form of a 6 and a half foot tall lime tree cake, with 20 kilos of edible bark, and hundreds of hand coloured rice paper leaves. Nastassja tackles a 4 and a half foot tall wedding cake, embellished with fondant flowers and edible lace, which must also include traditional Kente fabric patterns in its design as nod to the couple's Ghanaian heritage.

programme.title: Mary Makes Christmas
programme.desc: Mary brings festive cheer with delicious holiday recipes, from classic mince pies to show-stopping desserts. Join her for expert tips, festive fun, and mouthwatering seasonal delights.

programme.title: Nigella's Amsterdam Christmas
programme.desc: Nigella returns for a very special Christmas treat when she travels to Amsterdam to enjoy the festive season. Nigella takes viewers on visits to her favourite places in the Dutch capital city.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt and Maggie are taking the bakers back in time for retro week. The bakers must bake a Swiss roll within two hours in the signature challenge, and a Neenish tart for the technical bake.

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The second heat of bakers make mad-alien madeleines, and showcase their dream home in cake.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: A Nana has taken on three grandchildren all with very different dietary demands. Michael van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj are on the case to get this family to eat the same meal around a table.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas With Bells On
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver provides an inspiring collection of traditional Christmas classics, alongside new ideas and fantastic party food.

programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: Mary returns to one of her most loved locations, Port Isaac on the Cornwall coast. Along with the brilliant local Chef Nathan Outlaw she explores some of the wonderful classics inspired by the great outdoors - even if the weather is against them! From her take on a delicious Bouillabaisse filled with some of the ocean's best offerings, to a freshly caught Mackerel bbq'd by the sea, and some rich and crispy Spatchcock Poussin marinated and cooked alongside it. As the home cook and Michelin started chef cook alongside each other it is obvious how inspired they both are by the coast and a shared love of simple, fresh, local food cooked well.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon goes full-on to produce great hearty food that packs a deliciously robust punch. Recipes guaranteed to keep you cosy, warm and wonderfully well fed are superb Welsh rarebit and delectable roasted tomato soup.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Shortcuts
programme.desc: Jamie's recipes for a spectacular Christmas feast: the ultimate roast turkey, a show-stopping veggie centrepiece, and a stuffing to please both meat-eaters and vegetarians.

programme.title: Gordon, Gino & Fred Desperately Seeking
programme.desc: Gordon, Gino and Fred are getting together for another extraordinary culinary Christmas adventure. 2020 has been a bit bleak to say the least and the boys are keen to end the year with a bang. After Gino led them to the deserts of Morocco for last year's mis-adventure, Gordon is determined that they celebrate the season properly this time, so has arranged a boys trip away before the madness of a family xmas kicks in, to the ultimate winter wonderland and the official home of Santa. GGF are going to Lapland to experience a treasure trove of festive fare, to enjoy spectacular landscapes and meet Father Christmas in person. Standby for a unique Christmas cracker!

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Malt loaf has been a popular teatime treat for more than 80 years; these days we get through a staggering 130 million of them every year. So to get to grips with how this sweet and squidgy cake cum bread is made, Gregg Wallace is rolling up his sleeves to get stuck in, following a production line of massive dough mixing, mind-boggling tin filling and intensely hot baking. Meanwhile, historian Ruth Goodman reveals the surprising story of a British baking company that cooked up the first usiness computer, as well as visiting Cambridgeshire to find out how wheat flour was ground the traditional way, until the Victorians demand for white bread brought about the demise of Britain's iconic windmills.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: It's pastry week and with only six bakers left, the showstopper will bring out animal instincts. Matt and Maggie ask the bakers to make a 3D animal sculpture out of choux pastry.

programme.title: Jamie's Cracking Christmas
programme.desc: Time to deck those halls and spread a bit of Jamie's festive foodie cheer. It's a time to indulge in some of the most special food you'll eat all year.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Shortcuts
programme.desc: Jamie celebrates leftovers and transforms them into delicious dishes for between Christmas and New Year: A vegetable tarte tatin, Brussels sprouts Caesar salad and a spectacular trifle.

programme.desc: Nigella shows us how to have our most relaxed, most spectacular Christmas yet with her foolproof guide to stress-free festive cooking and easy entertaining.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Today, reputations are on the line, as 3 of our cake makers aim to impress at the world's most prestigious cake show, Cake International. Former tattoo artist Ben Cullen, battles to make a life sized Ringmaster Cake, standing over 6ft high, and covered in a whopping 15 kilos of detailed sugar paste. Molly hopes to make a splash with her 6ft square seascape, complete with a Mermaid covered in over 600 edible handmade scales. It's a race against time as Phil and Christine paint the final one of 5 famous works of art using cocoa butter on sugar paste live at the show, to complete their Sugar Art Gallery.

programme.desc: In order to make her smoky eggplant and cocoa dip, Justine sources some local cocoa in Mossman. At the Witches Chase, she makes an omelette with three types of cheese.

programme.desc: Embark on an enchanting journey with Justine as she revisits her roots, traversing the picturesque landscapes of southern France.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer encounters resistance in LA and Oakland to his Vegas expansion initiative. Joe takes a detour with Reggie; Jason blasts Kisan for his lack of transparency; Charles bristles over Larry's failure to give him credit for a lineman he landed; Ricky jumps to conclusions.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Desperate to score a key owner's blessing to bring pro football to Vegas, Spencer and Joe find themselves in a race against the clock involving a pair of equally critical meetings. Spurned by his new team mates, Ricky stages his own private workout, and finds roses aren't enough to appease an angry Amber. Vernon looks for a second chance; tensions between Charles and Larry escalate after a try out for Kisan.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer faces new challenges getting Wayne to finance his Vegas initiative, and ponders his place in the big picture. Looking to mend a long-festering feud between Larry and Coach Berg, Julie persuades Charles to invite both over for a home-cooked meal. Vernon is surprised by an unscheduled visit from a league official. Joe becomes obsessed with virtual reality. Ricky loses focus and pays the price.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: With the owners' meeting a week away, Spencer, Joe and Mr Anderson head north in search of financing for a Vegas stadium. Larry tests the limits of Charles' loyalty and patience; Ricky gets a sobering diagnosis, and presses Jason to close a deal.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer finds himself in damage-control mode after an incident involving Ricky and a brash high schooler. Excited over a possible plum job, Charles bristles over Julie's lack of disclosure at work. Looking to reduce Vernon's suspension, Reggie impresses Candace at the league office. Spencer and Joe find themselves at odds with Anderson over ASM's future.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer gets unexpected info that leads him to reassess his priorities. Joe considers the best way to break some bad news to the ASM staff. Marginalised once again by Siefert, Charles decides to take matters into his own hands.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer learns his master plan to bring a team to Las Vegas may be in jeopardy. Ricky arrives at a crossroads, personally and professionally. Charles sees his wish come true in Miami, but ends up with regrets.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer and Joe get a late game assist in their efforts to win over team owners. After some soul searching, Ricky maps out a new future. Jason gets Charles another crack at a coveted job. Reggie makes an impact at training camp.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer and Joe weigh the pros and cons of acquiring an extreme sport agency; Ricky sets down roots in LA with Amber and their daughter, but finds himself yearning for something else; Charles rallies a group of disgruntled employees as he begins his new tenure with the Rams.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer becomes immersed in a marketing controversy; still smarting from their feud in Miami, Charles agrees to give Ricky a chance, despite his better instincts.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer looks to rein in Lance's excesses; Vernon gets carried away at a ceremony retiring his number at his old LA high school, much to Reggie's dismay.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer and Joe find themselves in a crisis at SportsX. Ricky considers his motivations in ending his retirement. Jason turns to Donna for help.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer looks to elevate the SportsX TV brand, while Joe weighs a new offer. At Jayda's insistence, Spencer meets Quincy. Ricky tries to prove to Kisan he's still got the goods, while Charles is encouraged by Julie to bring more flair to his job.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer recruits Q to help him leverage a deal. With Reggie in tow, Joe talks business with some extreme skateboarders, collectively known as Illegal Civilization. Charles hosts a pre-free-agent reception.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer calls on the Anderson Brothers for help. Joe pitches Illegal Civilization to a major shoe brand. Ricky loses his cool at a neighbour's pool party. Julie accuses an overworked Charles of being afraid to succeed.

programme.title: Ballers
programme.desc: Spencer takes a big risk with the potential to blow the deal. After a heart-to-heart with Ricky, Charles spells out his vision for the team. Joe and Reggie enlist Vernon and Terrell "Sizzle" Suggs, to persuade Illegal Civilization.

programme.sub-title: There's No Place Like Home, Baby
programme.desc: Spencer takes his fight to the next level; Ricky jeopardises his comeback.

programme.desc: Spencer is shaken out of peaceful retirement by a tantalising offer; Joe and Lance take a big gamble on the future of Sports X; Ricky lands in hot water.

programme.desc: Spencer goes to the Anderson brothers for financial help; Joe is tasked with an apology at Sports X; Ricky and Vernon each consider new futures.

programme.desc: Jason is caught between competing offers from Spencer and Joe; Vernon has a tryout at an eSports company; Ricky reverts to his partying ways.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Aspiring comedian Pete finds himself at a crossroads after an emotional crisis. While attempting to find solace at a New York comedy club, he befriends cynical comedy legend Artie Lange, who reluctantly offers him some sage advice and a place to crash.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: While considering his next move, literally and figuratively, Pete is put on the spot when Artie enlists his help in keeping him clean and sober during a road show in Albany with co-headliner TJ Miller. Later, Pete concocts a plan to keep Artie away from the temptations of an ardent fan.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete comes to a startling realisation while staying at TJ Miller's place: that the comic has a hard time 'turning it off'. Later, the two head upstate to Jess's yard sale, where Pete teams up with an unexpected ally to get back a prized possession.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: In an effort to win some stage time at a comedy club, Pete spends the night 'barking', handing out flyers to passers-by, on the streets of New York. He finds himself encountering disinterested locals, befriending some up-and-coming comics, engaging in a turf war with a rival barker, and getting a pep talk from a veteran.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: When Pete's parents decide to come to New York for his mother's birthday, Pete begs Jess to keep up the charade of their marriage. But an incredibly tense dinner reveals some harsh truths about their relationships.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: While guest appearing on Artie's podcast, Pete meets a sympathetic Sarah Silverman, who invites him to stay with her and her collection of stray comedians. At Sarah's suggestion, Pete gets a gig as a 'warm-up' comic, and ends up being tasked with saving the day.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete's warm-up gig on Rachael Ray's show gives him a natural high, but he comes crashing down after a surprise visit from Leif's wife, who pulls him back into the chaos of his separation from Jess.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: After attempt to cheer up Pete goes wrong, Artie tags along with Pete to a baptism. Artie gets insight into religion and God from a woman, and Pete runs into Jess which makes him question how much he is willing to sacrifice for a life in comedy.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete has a chance encounter with famed magician Penn Jillette, who shakes his faith and inspires a questioning Pete to try living with abandon for one night.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete decides to spend the day with Leif, who encourages him to be open to the universe. Their carefree attitude is challenged when the two men find themselves at odds and in trouble with the law.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: When Pete is abandoned by Artie, he is rescued by comedian Bill Burr, who takes pity on him and offers up his guestroom. Bill encourages Pete to toughen up and embrace his masculine side as these two become unlikely friends.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete tries to be supportive when a fellow comic at The Boston gets his big break. Later, Pete reconnects with Jess, who encourages him to find a healthy way to express himself.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete reunites with Ali, who takes him on a tour of New York's alternative comedy scene, where they encounter a wide array of comedians and venues over the course of one night.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete's decision to organise a secret benefit for an ailing comedy vet finds him scrambling to put on a good show and being forced to face some hard truths about Artie.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: On a trip to NACA, a comedy conference to book college gigs, Pete and Ali find themselves having wildly different experiences dealing with a very particular audience, and a recognisable third wheel.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: In an attempt to show Ali that he has a backbone, Pete enters a comedy-roast battle, but finds that being mean is harder than he thought. The fierce competition brings some uncomfortable truths to the surface, forcing Pete to examine his priorities in life.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: As his successful two-month college comedy tour comes to an end, Pete, donning a new look and confidence to match, takes a college-aged aspiring comedian, Jaboukie, under his wing. But Pete's return to New York finds him having an awkward reunion and a big audition at the Comedy Cellar that could change everything.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Feeling like his time away from NY has taken him out of the scene, Pete takes an offer to perform at a Jewish show where Elon Gold and Modi question Pete's impulsive behavior with a woman he met earlier that day.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete falls headfirst into a relationship with his new girlfriend Kat, who encourages him to be more aggressive in his career. On the night of their three-week anniversary, things amp up after a visit to a sex shop and a comedy show that morphs into a night all about Pete.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete travels to New Jersey with Jason to do a weekend of shows at a big corporate comedy club and is surprised to find his ex Ali staying at the comedy condo with them. Jason's misogynistic act and point of view causes Pete to question his path in comedy.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: During a tour stop, Pete pays a visit to his parents and introduces them to Kat, who notices the uncomfortably close relationship he has with his overbearing mom. As he tries to keep the peace, Pete grows increasingly trapped between the two most important women in his life.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete and Kat attend a viewing party for Ali's late-night spot on Seth Meyers. Tensions mount as Ali is nervous about how her set went and Kat becomes jealous of Pete's friendship with his ex.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: While enjoying the perks of being on a Christian comedy tour, Pete feels busted when he runs into NY Cellar comic Jessica Kirson in Charleston. After ghosting Kat while on the road, Pete finally sits down to talk about their relationship.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete jumps at the opportunity of a lifetime to open for John Mulaney at Town Hall, but the gig leaves him in an awkward situation.

He finds himself encountering disinterested locals, befriending some up-and-coming comics, engaging in a turf war with a rival barker, and getting a pep talk from a veteran.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: When Pete's parents decide to come to New York for his mother's birthday, Pete begs Jess to keep up the charade of their marriage. But an incredibly tense dinner reveals some harsh truths about their relationships.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete has a chance encounter with famed magician Penn Jillette, who shakes his faith and inspires a questioning Pete to try living with abandon for one night.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete decides to spend the day with Leif, who encourages him to be open to the universe. Their carefree attitude is challenged when the two men find themselves at odds and in trouble with the law.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: When Pete is abandoned by Artie, he is rescued by comedian Bill Burr, who takes pity on him and offers up his guestroom. Bill encourages Pete to toughen up and embrace his masculine side as these two become unlikely friends.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete tries to be supportive when a fellow comic at The Boston gets his big break. Later, Pete reconnects with Jess, who encourages him to find a healthy way to express himself.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete reunites with Ali, who takes him on a tour of New York's alternative comedy scene, where they encounter a wide array of comedians and venues over the course of one night.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete's decision to organise a secret benefit for an ailing comedy vet finds him scrambling to put on a good show and being forced to face some hard truths about Artie.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: On a trip to NACA, a comedy conference to book college gigs, Pete and Ali find themselves having wildly different experiences dealing with a very particular audience, and a recognisable third wheel.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: In an attempt to show Ali that he has a backbone, Pete enters a comedy-roast battle, but finds that being mean is harder than he thought. The fierce competition brings some uncomfortable truths to the surface, forcing Pete to examine his priorities in life.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: As his successful two-month college comedy tour comes to an end, Pete, donning a new look and confidence to match, takes a college-aged aspiring comedian, Jaboukie, under his wing. But Pete's return to New York finds him having an awkward reunion and a big audition at the Comedy Cellar that could change everything.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Feeling like his time away from NY has taken him out of the scene, Pete takes an offer to perform at a Jewish show where Elon Gold and Modi question Pete's impulsive behavior with a woman he met earlier that day.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete falls headfirst into a relationship with his new girlfriend Kat, who encourages him to be more aggressive in his career. On the night of their three-week anniversary, things amp up after a visit to a sex shop and a comedy show that morphs into a night all about Pete.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete travels to New Jersey with Jason to do a weekend of shows at a big corporate comedy club and is surprised to find his ex Ali staying at the comedy condo with them. Jason's misogynistic act and point of view causes Pete to question his path in comedy.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: During a tour stop, Pete pays a visit to his parents and introduces them to Kat, who notices the uncomfortably close relationship he has with his overbearing mom. As he tries to keep the peace, Pete grows increasingly trapped between the two most important women in his life.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete and Kat attend a viewing party for Ali's late-night spot on Seth Meyers. Tensions mount as Ali is nervous about how her set went and Kat becomes jealous of Pete's friendship with his ex.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: While enjoying the perks of being on a Christian comedy tour, Pete feels busted when he runs into NY Cellar comic Jessica Kirson in Charleston. After ghosting Kat while on the road, Pete finally sits down to talk about their relationship.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete jumps at the opportunity of a lifetime to open for John Mulaney at Town Hall, but the gig leaves him in an awkward situation.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: At a bohemian party, Jemaine falls for Sally (who used to date Bret), but Bret spoils the moment. Meanwhile, Murray shares his two-pronged plan for the band.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Desperate for cash, Bret and Jemaine get jobs as human billboards to make ends meet. However, it interferes with the bands gig at a travel expo so Murray and Jemaine replace Bret with a cassette tape.

programme.desc: Still adjusting to new-found responsibilities, one of Issa's exes finds himself torn between two places, while an important woman in his life remains wary of his ability to be fully present back home.

programme.desc: Issa and her new man tread awkward waters at a work-related social event. Meanwhile, Molly juggles multiple suitors, and Issa plays defence against online attacks about her business.

programme.desc: A family emergency finds Molly stretched thin between taking care of loved ones, staying on top of work, and zeroing in on her own perceived shortcomings. Issa and her new man grow closer than ever.

programme.desc: A distracted Issa mulls over work and love as she stumbles through the consequences of her falling out with a colleague.

programme.desc: A long overdue girls' night filled with self-reflection and real talk puts things into perspective for Issa and her friends.

programme.desc: At a crossroads in her career, Issa weighs the pros and cons of two viable paths while simultaneously contemplating an important personal next step.

programme.title: Insecure
programme.desc: As she and her friends try to make time for each other no matter what, Issa looks back on all it took to get to where she is today.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Grappling with the loss of her sister, Sam begins her journey to discover herself and a community of outsiders who don't fit in but don't give up, showing that finding your people is possible, anywhere, somewhere.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Sam begins to bond with fellow members of choir practice, but her burgeoning friendship with Joel is threatened over competing outlooks on their future in Kansas.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Sam and Joel embark on a covert mission to uncover the source of her brother-in-law Rick's sudden windfall. Choir practice provides Sam with the courage and clarity to share mounting concerns.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: After Pastor Deb asks for an impromptu meeting, Joel prepares for the worst. With money tight, Sam gets creative in sourcing a solution for the upcoming corn harvest.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: With a tornado looming, Sam prepares for a different kind of storm as she shares the truth about Rick with Tricia. After impulsively adopting a dog, Joel realises he wants more out of life.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: After a surprising update concerning her recent job performance, Sam must evaluate her next step. Meanwhile, Tricia and Charity's working relationship grows complicated.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: In her quest to reinspire Joel, Sam recruits a small team to help and reveals a new potential location for choir practice. Later, on a purple party bus, Sam realises just how far she's come.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: While he rents out his house, Joel and Sam revel in the highs of their temporary living 'situationship' and the lows of cleaning up after strangers.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: At poker night, Fred throws Sam, Joel, and Tiffani a curveball. Meanwhile, Sam attends a former voice teacher's concert in hopes of becoming her student again with unforeseen consequences.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: As Mary Jo's behaviour worsens, Sam and Tricia grapple with the difficult decision of escalating her care.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: After accidentally blowing his own cover stalking Brad, Joel opens up about his personal crisis stemming from Fred's upcoming wedding.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: While celebrating Tricia's birthday, Sam is blindsided by surprising new information about Holly. As Charity's lies continue to affect Tricia's social life, Joel makes a move with Brad, which leads to a misstep with Sam.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Distancing herself from Joel, Sam concentrates on helping Tricia with her blossoming pillow business - which soon faces sabotage from Charity. Meanwhile, Fred attempts to play peacemaker with Sam and Joel.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: After reuniting with Joel at a funeral, Sam takes stock of herself and her actions - and decides to make things right with her best friend.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Sam, a true Kansan on the surface, struggles to fit her hometown's mould. Grappling with loss and acceptance, singing is her saving grace and leads her on a journey to discover herself.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Overwhelmed by the amount of change happening in the lives of her loved ones, Sam is hit especially hard by a friend's brutally honest comment; Sam helps Brad with a special task.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Tricia shakes things up at the country club; Joel and Brad navigate their new normal; Sam finally sees a doctor.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: An unexpected issue arises on Sam and Tricia's trip to Kansas City for a trade show. Joel reluctantly attends Brad's bible book club and runs into a complicated figure from his past.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Surrounded by her sister and chosen family on Thanksgiving, Sam gains a better appreciation of what it means to love.

programme.desc: Attending an elaborate launch party, Richard and his computer programmer friends, Big Head, Dinesh and Gilfoyle, dream of making it big. Instead, they're living in a communal Hacker Hostel.

programme.desc: Peter Gregory demands a formal business plan for Pied Piper. Meanwhile Richard hires Jared, a former Hooli employee and they set about trying to trim the fat.

programme.title: The Killer
programme.desc: A feared contract killer, known as "The Queen of the Dead" in Paris' criminal underworld, who refuses to murder a young blind woman on the orders of her handler, finds herself hunted by old colleagues and a determined detective.

programme.title: Dune: Part Two
programme.desc: Dune: Part Two will explore the mythic journey of Paul Atreides as he unites with Chani and the Fremen while on a warpath of revenge against the conspirators who destroyed his family. Facing a choice between the love of his life and the fate of the known universe, he endeavors to prevent a terrible future only he can foresee.

programme.title: Longlegs
programme.desc: In pursuit of a serial killer, an FBI agent uncovers a series of occult clues that she must solve to end his terrifying killing spree.

programme.title: Expend4bles
programme.desc: Armed with every weapon they can get their hands on and the skills to use them, The Expendables are the world's last line of defense and the team that gets called when all other options are off the table. But new team members with new styles and tactics are going to give "new blood" a new meaning.

programme.title: Ezra
